notes.note: The letter-heading. PROPR. = pro praetore, propraetor of Asia (51-50 BC). One of Cicero's cluster of recommendations to Thermus (Fam. 13.53-57), this one a follow-up thanking him for favours already granted.

notes.anchor: the son of my friend and interpreter
notes.note: An interpres was an official interpreter attached to a provincial governor's staff, indispensable in Greek-speaking and multilingual provinces. The elder Marcilius served Cicero in this role during his Cilician proconsulship; Cicero recommends his son M. Marcilius.

notes.anchor: that the young man's mother-in-law be not made defendant
notes.note: Cicero asks the governor to prevent the young man's mother-in-law from being made a defendant (rea), i.e. brought to trial in the provincial court. Provincial governors held wide judicial discretion over which suits proceeded against whom.

notes.anchor: in his long term of attendance
notes.note: Apparitio is the formal service of an apparitor or staff-attendant to a Roman magistrate. Cicero's praise of the elder Marcilius's loyalty, restraint (abstinentia) and modesty is the language of an ideal subordinate official.
